120.reason: [This method assumes the char constant always has exactly one character between quotes and a closing quote; it does not validate length or handle escape sequences, and will crash if input ends prematurely (e.g., "'a" or "'")]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This method assumes the char constant always has exactly one character between quotes and a closing quote; it does not validate length or handle escape sequences, and will crash if input ends prematurely (e.g., "'a" or "'")]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This method assumes the char constant always has exactly one character between quotes and a closing quote; it does not validate length or handle escape sequences, but more critically, it accesses input.charAt(pos) without checking bounds after incrementing pos, which may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException or incorrect parsing]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This method assumes the char constant always has exactly one character between quotes and a closing quote; it does not validate length or handle escape sequences, and may crash if input ends prematurely (e.g., "'a" or "'")]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [This method assumes the char constant always has exactly one character between quotes and a closing quote; it does not validate length or handle missing closing quote, which may cause IndexOutOfBoundsException or incorrect parsing]

242.reason: [After detecting an error (single '&' or '|'), the lexer should stop further tokenization per problem statement (only one lexical error per file), but it continues processing; also, writing to error.txt inside getSymbol causes repeated writes if multiple lines are processed]

reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [After detecting an error (single '&' or '|'), the lexer should stop further tokenization per problem statement (only one lexical error per file), but it continues processing; also, writing to error.txt inside getSymbol causes repeated writes if multiple lines are processed]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [When encountering a single '&' or '|', the code reports error 'a' correctly, but it does not advance the lexer state properly—it leaves 'pos' pointing past the invalid symbol without skipping it in a way that avoids reprocessing, and more importantly, it writes to error.txt immediately inside getSymbol, which may cause multiple writes if called per line; also, error.put(1, 'a') uses hardcoded key 1 instead of lineNumber]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-3: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [After detecting an error (single '&' or '|'), the lexer should stop further tokenization for this line or file as per problem statement (only one lexical error per test), but it continues processing. Also, writing to error.txt inside getSymbol breaks design—error output should happen once at the end, not per error occurrence. Moreover, error.put(1, 'a') uses fixed key 1 instead of lineNumber.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-4: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [After detecting an error (e.g., single '&' or '|'), the lexer should stop further tokenization as per problem statement (only one lexical error per file), but it continues processing the rest of the line or file, possibly causing undefined behavior or multiple outputs]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-sc-5: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [After detecting an error (single '&' or '|'), the lexer should stop further tokenization as per problem statement (only one lexical error per file), but it continues processing the rest of the line, which may cause undefined behavior or additional incorrect output]
